sections.title: Market Pulse: Indices Close Lower as Tech Weighs, Inflation Cools

items.headline: U.S. Equities Dip as Growth Sectors Underperform Amid Cooling Inflation Data
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed lower on Friday, July 18, 2026, with major indices recording declines. Technology and other growth sectors experienced a sell-off, contributing significantly to the overall market downturn. This comes as fresh economic data indicated a cooling in U.S. inflation for June, potentially easing pressure on the Federal Reserve regarding interest rate policy. However, concerns persist over the inflationary impact of the massive AI infrastructure build-out.

items.statContext: Down 1.05% today. The index fell 1.55% for the week ending July 17.
Fell 1.40% today. The Nasdaq fell 2.9% for the week ending July 17.

items.headline: Forte Biosciences Leads Monthly Gains
items.summary: Forte Biosciences, Inc. continues its strong performance, emerging as the best-performing stock in July 2026, driven by sustained market momentum in the healthcare sector.

items.headline: PayPal Shows Strong Monthly Uptick
items.summary: PayPal Holdings, Inc. recorded significant gains this month, reflecting positive sentiment and potentially strong underlying business performance.

items.headline: Intuitive Surgical Plunges on Growth Concerns
items.summary: Intuitive Surgical Inc. saw a sharp decline yesterday, following news of moderated growth in its da Vinci robotic surgery system in the U.S., impacting investor confidence.

items.headline: Netflix Drops After Mixed Q2 Results
items.summary: Netflix Inc. shares fell significantly yesterday after reporting mixed second-quarter fiscal results, with earnings beating expectations but sales missing and Q3 guidance falling short of market forecasts.

items.headline: SanDisk Continues Weekly Decline
items.summary: SanDisk experienced a substantial drop this week, making it one of the worst-performing stocks, potentially due to broader tech sector weakness or specific company news.

items.headline: U.S. Inflation Cools in June, Federal Reserve Holds Steady
items.summary: The U.S. consumer inflation cooled in June, with overall prices dropping 0.4% from May—the largest monthly decline in four years. The yearly inflation rate fell to 3.5%, reinforcing expectations that the Federal Reserve will likely keep interest rates unchanged through the second half of 2026. The Fed entered its pre-meeting silence ahead of today's decision to hold rates steady.

items.headline: Energy and Real Estate Lead, Technology and Basic Materials Lag
items.summary: For the week ending July 17, the Energy sector was the best performer, gaining 4.54%, followed by Real Estate, up 2.64%. In contrast, Technology and Basic Materials were the weakest sectors, declining by 3.65% and 1.85% respectively. This indicates a potential shift in market leadership and investor focus.

items.headline: AI Infrastructure Build-Out Poses Inflationary Pressure
items.summary: Despite cooling inflation, economists warn that the massive investment in AI infrastructure, including data centers, memory chips, processors, and electricity, could exert renewed inflationary pressure through the end of the year. This could potentially lead the Federal Reserve to consider lifting interest rates later in 2026.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season in Focus Amid High Expectations
items.summary: The Q2 2026 earnings season is a key focus for investors, with S&P 500 earnings growth estimated at a robust 23.6% year-over-year. Energy and Information Technology are expected to be leading sectors in earnings growth. However, individual company performance, such as Netflix's mixed results, highlights the risk of not meeting elevated investor expectations.
